display.purpose: Public hero and bid-page opener only — the one place the system raises its voice.

headline.purpose: Page titles; italic <em> renders in Leather Tan.

title.purpose: Section and card titles; H2 may carry the 36px tan underline.

body.purpose: Default reading text in Olive; cap prose at 65–75ch.

label.purpose: Uppercase tracked microtype — form labels, kickers, the nameplate voice.

shadows.name: soft
shadows.purpose: Resting elevation for cards and panels — a barely-there lift off the paper.

shadows.name: lift
shadows.purpose: Hover/active and pulled-forward surfaces; pairs with translateY(-2px).

motion.name: transition-fast
motion.purpose: Button/input state changes — color and border shifts.

motion.name: transition-slow
motion.purpose: Larger reveals on the public/bid surfaces.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The committing action — submit a booking, send a bid, pay a deposit.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; font-family: var(--sans, Inter, sans-serif); font-weight: 600; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 2.5px; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 12px 20px; color: var(--cream, #e8e4d5); background: var(--olive, #3f4a21); border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 2px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--olive-deep, #2a3216); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--tan-deep, #9a8159); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: The quieter, intentionally lighter alternate action — sentence case, softer tracking.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; font-family: var(--sans, Inter, sans-serif); font-weight: 500; font-size: 15px; letter-spacing: 1.5px; padding: 12px 20px; color: var(--olive, #3f4a21); background: var(--paper, #fbfaf3); border: 1px solid var(--border-strong, rgba(63,74,33,0.28)); border-radius: 2px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease, border-color 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: var(--paper-warm, #f5f1e5); border-color: var(--tan-deep, #9a8159); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--tan-deep, #9a8159); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Lowest-weight action — tan text, faint wash on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; font-family: var(--sans, Inter, sans-serif); font-weight: 600; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 2.5px; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 12px 20px; color: var(--tan-deep, #9a8159); background: transparent; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 2px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease, color 150ms ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { color: var(--olive, #3f4a21); background: rgba(184,156,115,0.08); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--tan-deep, #9a8159); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Default content container — flat on the paper, soft resting shadow, olive hairline.
components.html:
Morning Upland Hunt

A guided half-day over pointing dogs. Gear and instruction included.
components.css: .ds-card { background: var(--paper, #fbfaf3); border: 1px solid var(--border, rgba(63,74,33,0.15)); border-radius: 3px; padding: 24px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(31,38,17,0.04), 0 8px 24px rgba(31,38,17,0.06); transition: transform 240ms ease, box-shadow 240ms ease; } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(31,38,17,0.06), 0 16px 48px rgba(31,38,17,0.12); border-color: rgba(184,156,115,0.2); } .ds-card-title { font-family: var(--serif, 'Cormorant Garamond', serif); font-weight: 600; font-size: 24px; line-height: 1.15; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: var(--olive, #3f4a21); margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-card-body { font-family: var(--sans, Inter, sans-serif); font-size: 15px; line-height: 1.6; color: var(--olive, #3f4a21); margin: 0; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Paper-warm fill, strong-olive border, calm two-property focus shift (no glow).
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; padding: 12px 14px; font-family: var(--sans, Inter, sans-serif); font-size: 15px; color: var(--olive, #3f4a21); background: var(--paper-warm, #f5f1e5); border: 1px solid var(--border-strong, rgba(63,74,33,0.28)); border-radius: 2px; outline: none; transition: border-color 150ms ease, background 150ms ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--gray, #6b6557); opacity: 0.7; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: var(--tan-deep, #9a8159); background: var(--paper, #fbfaf3); }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Booking/adventure state — uppercase micro, tinted background plus matching darker text. Color always backed by the word.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.4em; font-family: var(--sans, Inter, sans-serif); font-size: 9.5px; letter-spacing: 1.5px; text-transform: uppercase; font-weight: 600; padding: 4px 9px; border-radius: 2px; line-height: 1.4; border: 1px solid transparent; } .ds-badge-open { background: rgba(63,110,50,0.12); color: #2d5520; } .ds-badge-full { background: rgba(140,50,50,0.10); color: #8b3030; }

components.name: Alert
components.description: Severity-tinted, fully-bordered notice with an uppercase micro title. The one sanctioned heavier edge.
components.html:
Heads up

This date is held for 48 hours while the guest reviews the bid.
components.css: .ds-alert { display: flex; gap: 12px; align-items: flex-start; text-align: left; border: 1px solid; border-left-width: 4px; border-radius: 2px; padding: 16px; font-family: var(--sans, Inter, sans-serif); font-size: 13px; color: var(--olive, #3f4a21); line-height: 1.5; } .ds-alert-info { background: rgba(47,84,130,0.07); border-color: rgba(47,84,130,0.25); } .ds-alert-title { display: block; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 2px; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--accent-info, #2f5482); margin-bottom: 0.25rem; } .ds-alert-body p { margin: 0; }

components.name: Eyebrow
components.description: Signature nameplate microtype — uppercase tracked tan kicker. A deliberate named system element, not a per-section reflex.
components.css: .ds-eyebrow { display: block; font-family: var(--sans, Inter, sans-serif); font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 3px; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--tan-deep, #9a8159); font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.4; }

narrative.overview: Rhythm Outdoors looks like a well-kept private land-and-lodge tradition rendered for the screen: olive fields, tan leather, cream correspondence. The surface is the warm near-white of good paper; the ink is a deep field olive; the single ornament is a worn-leather tan. Serif headings carry the cadence of an engraved nameplate, and the sans body reads like a clean typed entry beneath it. Nothing shouts. The authority comes from restraint and from the sense that every detail has been looked after — the same calm confidence a guest needs before handing over a deposit, and the same quiet order a staff member needs to run three clubs from one screen. This is a product-register system at its core (admin, booking funnel, portals are working surfaces where the tool disappears into the task), but the guest-facing public site and the bid page are conversion surfaces where the same materials are allowed to breathe more.
keyCharacteristics: Warm-paper canvas (#fbfaf3), deep-olive ink (#3f4a21), tan (#9a8159) as the one accent.
Cormorant Garamond serif for headings (with an italic tan emphasis); Inter for everything that does work.
Soft, olive-tinted elevation; flat by default, lift on intent.
Uppercase, widely-tracked sans labels and eyebrows as the engraved-nameplate voice.
Quiet by default; the bid page and public hero are where the system is allowed to open up.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Tan is the only accent. It earns attention by scarcity — links, focus, eyebrows, a single hairline rule. The moment a second decorative hue appears, the estate starts to look like a brochure.

rules.name: The Olive-Line Rule
rules.body: Every border and divider is semi-transparent olive (rgba(63,74,33,…)), not gray. Lines belong to the brand's hue; a neutral-gray border is a tell that a component skipped the system.

rules.name: The Serif-Speaks Rule
rules.body: Serif is for headings, the lead paragraph, and italic emphasis — never for UI controls, labels, data, or buttons. A display face in a button label is forbidden.

rules.name: The Italic-Tan Rule
rules.body: Emphasis inside a heading is <em> rendered italic in Tan, not bold and not a color swap on the whole line. One word leans in; the rest holds the olive.

rules.name: The Flat-Paper Rule
rules.body: Surfaces rest flat on the paper. The Lift shadow appears only as a response to state (hover, focus, deliberate emphasis) — if everything is lifted, nothing is.

rules.name: The Olive-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadows are tinted olive-black, never neutral rgba(0,0,0,…). A pure-black shadow on warm paper reads cold and off-system.

dos: Do keep Tan as the single accent (The One Accent Rule) — links, focus, eyebrows, one hairline. Scarcity is the point.
Do draw every border and divider in semi-transparent olive, never neutral gray (The Olive-Line Rule).
Do reserve Cormorant for headings, the lead paragraph, and italic emphasis; everything functional is Inter (The Serif-Speaks Rule).
Do keep surfaces flat on the paper and raise the Lift shadow only on state (The Flat-Paper Rule); tint shadows olive-black.
Do verify contrast: body text ≥ 4.5:1. Stone Gray (#6b6557) on Paper sits near the floor — use Olive for body and reserve Gray for genuinely secondary micro text; placeholders must still clear 4.5:1.
Do pair every status color with its word and/or an icon — never encode meaning in color alone.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion: the global baseline near-zeroes animation; opt a critical entrance back in only as a fade/crossfade.
Do let the public hero and bid page open up (bigger serif, more air); keep admin and funnel dense and quiet.
donts: Don't build a generic SaaS dashboard — no gray-card sameness, no identical icon-heading-text grids repeated down a page.
Don't ship a loud OTA booking site — no countdown timers, 'only 3 left!' badges, discount confetti, manufactured scarcity, or hard-sell banners.
Don't drift into corporate hotel chain blandness — no sterile, focus-grouped, stock-photo filler with no opinion.
Don't reach for the trendy AI-startup kit — no purple gradients, no glassmorphism, no background-clip:text gradient text, no big-number hero-metric template.

Don't use a colored border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a side-stripe accent on cards, list items, or callouts (the alert's heavier edge inside a fully-bordered box is the one sanctioned exception).
Don't put a display/serif face on UI controls, labels, data, or buttons.
Don't use pure-white surfaces or rgba(0,0,0,…) shadows — both read cold against warm paper.
Don't nest cards, and don't render dropdowns with position:absolute inside an overflow:hidden/auto container (they clip).
